title: tephrosia deflexa
intro: Tephrosia deflexa, also known as the "wild sensitive plant," is a perennial legume native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Tephrosia deflexa include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create a medicinal tea or poultice, although its use and preparation may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
